result.markdown: --- execute: # Default settings for code blocks warning: false # Do not print warnings message: false # Do not print messages size: "scriptsize" # Set code size to script size format: pdf: # Specify .pdf formatting options documentclass: article # Document Class number-sections: true # Number Sections reference-section-title: "References" # Title for reference section code-line-numbers: true # Line numbers printed in code cap-location: top # Captions at the top of tables geometry: - top=0.75in - left=0.75in - bottom=0.75in - right=0.75in format-links: [pdf] --- \normalsize ::: {.cell} ::: \normalsize # Ingredients \normalsize - 6 Stella D’oro breakfast treats - 1/3 cup sugar - 1/3 cup butter (melted) - 3 5.3 oz vanilla yogurt cups - 1 cup cream cheese (not whipped) - 2 eggs - 2/3 cup sugar - 1/4 cup flour - 1/4 teaspoon salt - 1 heaping teaspoon vanilla bean paste - Various fresh berries, cut into bite-size pieces - 1 lb chicken thighs or extra firm tofu - 10 oz bag of frozen corn - 1 cup brown rice - 1/2 cup quinoa - 1 can of black beans - 1/2 cup shredded cheddar jack or Mexican cheese - Cholula Cilantro Lime Cremosa Sauce - 2 roma tomatoes - 1 lime, quartered - 0.5 oz fresh cilantro - 1 bag baby arugula (regular is fine, too) - 1 pound pasta (e.g., bucatini or farfalle) - 6-8 ounces of fresh or jarred pesto - 1 lemon - Grated parmesan cheese - Pine nuts - 1 cup rice - 8 oz mushrooms - 3 garlic cloves - 8 oz sugar snap peas - 2 tsp gochujang - 2 tablespoon mayonnaise - 3 tsp sesame oil - 5 oz spinach - 4 eggs - 1 cup kimchi \normalsize \newpage # Recipe One \normalsize 1. Add brown rice and quinoa to 3 cups of salted water, bring to a boil, reduce to simmer for 15-20 minutes. When done, mix in lime zest, juice from one lime wedge, salt and pepper. 1. Dice roma tomatoes, zest and quarter lime, and roughly chop the fresh cilantro. 1. Season chicken/tofy with salt and pepper. Cook chicken/tofu, transfer to cutting board, and cut into bite size pieces. 1. Drain and rinse black beans. 1. Cook corn and beans together in a frying pan. If you choose to microwave the corn, you can add beans to the rice-quinoa mix when it is almost done. 1. Add rice-quinoa mix into each bowl. Top with chicken, corn, beans, cheese, tomato, cilantro lime cremosa sauce, fresh cilantro, and a lime wedge. \normalsize \newpage # Recipe Two \normalsize 1. Boil and cook pasta according to box, strain but reserve 1 cup pasta water. 1. In the stock pot used to cook the pasta, add two tablespoons of olive oil and whisk in pesto over medium heat. 1. Whisk in 1/2 cup pasta water, a little more if needed to create a light sauce. 1. Remove sauce from heat and toss cooked pasta in the sauce. 1. Stir arugula in and cover until wilted. 1. Add juice from half a lemon, add more to taste. 1. Serve with parmesan cheese and pine nuts. \normalsize \newpage # Recipe Three \normalsize 1. Cook 1/2 cup rice according to package instructions. 1. Thinly slice mushrooms, mince garlic. Trim stem ends and strings from snap peas. 1. Combine garlic, gochujang, 1 tsp sesame oil, and mayonnaise. Mix in water 1 tsp at a time until sauce can be drizzled. 1. Cook mushrooms in a bit of oil until lightly browned. 1. Add sugar snap peas and cook for 3-4 minutes. 1. Add spinach and cook until wilted. 1. Add 2 tsp sesame oil and a pinch of salt and pepper. 1. Fry 4 eggs. 1. Divide rice, kimchi, stirfried vegetables, and eggs between bowls. Drizzle with gochujang sauce. \normalsize \newpage # Breakfast Recipe \normalsize 1. Place Stella D’oro cookies into a Ziploc bag and pulverize. 1. Combine cookie dust, sugar, and melted butter and combine. 1. Push the crust into the bottom of an 8 or 9 inch springform pan. 1. Preheat oven to 325F. 1. Mix the vanilla yogurt cups, cream cheese, eggs, sugar, flour, salt, vanilla bean paste together until smooth. A blender works best, but a hand mixer can work. 1. Pour yogurt mixture on top of the crust in the springform pan. 1. Bake for 55 minutes. Cook a few more minutes if the middle is not yet set. 1. Cool on the counter for about two hours, and then overnight in the refrigerator. 1. Top with berries and serve. \normalsize
